The Real Problem

Most PT Treats the Pain.
We Rebuild the Person.

If you've done PT before and it didn't stick, it's not because PT doesn't work. It's because most clinics stop when the pain stops — not when you're actually strong again.

🩹

The Surface Problem

You're in pain, losing strength, or recovering from surgery. You've tried chiro, massage, maybe injections. Still not where you want to be.

😔

How It Feels

Frustrated. A little scared. Worried PT won't work this time. Like your body is starting to betray you — and nobody has a real plan to stop it.

⚖️

Why It's Just Wrong

People who've worked hard their whole lives shouldn't have to slow down just because most PT clinics treat the pain and send them home without rebuilding the strength that keeps them going.

What We Do

Everything You Need
to Move Well and Stay Strong

From acute injury to peak performance — we cover the full spectrum of movement health.

🦾
Physical Therapy
Hands-on, one-on-one care with Doctors of PT. The Muscle Forward approach — not just exercise sheets.
Learn more →
🏋️
Strength Training
Real strength work supervised by clinicians who understand how your body moves — and what holds it back.
Learn more →
🧘
Active Stretch
Assisted flexibility and mobility work to keep you moving freely between PT sessions and for the long haul.
Learn more →
🏃
Running
Running analysis and programming to keep you on the road — faster, pain-free, and for years to come.
Learn more →
🎯
Performance Therapies

Learn more →
📊

How It Works

Three Steps to Moving
the Way You Want to Move

01

Evaluate

We get a full picture of what's going on and why — not just where it hurts. A thorough evaluation sets the foundation for everything that follows.

02

Treat & Rebuild

Hands-on care plus real strength work — not just exercises on a sheet. We treat the problem and build the body that prevents it from coming back.

03

Maintain

A path to stay strong for life — Active Strength, Active Stretch, and movement screens so you're never starting from scratch again.

Schedule Your First Visit →
Team Photo
Replace with real image
Your Guide

We've Been There.
That's Why We Do This.

Our therapists didn't become clinicians because it sounded good on paper. Whether it was a personal injury, a surgery that went longer than expected, or watching someone they love struggle to get back to the life they wanted — this team gets it from the inside out.

We don't just treat bodies. We treat people we understand. People who want to play with their grandkids, get back in the gym, finish the race, or just walk up the stairs without wincing.

Founded in 2002. Three locations in Baltimore County. 32,000+ visits in 2025. Doctors of Physical Therapy delivering hands-on, one-on-one care — with results patients talk about by name.

Founded 2002Doctors of PTPerry HallLuthervilleJacksonvilleMuscle Forward

Free Movement Screen

Not Sure Where to Start?
Book a Free Screen First.

A free 20-minute movement screen with one of our Doctors of PT. No charge, no commitment. You'll leave knowing exactly what's going on and what to do about it.

  • ✓  Full movement assessment
  • ✓  Clear explanation of your findings
  • ✓  A specific plan to move forward

Book Your Free Screen

We'll follow up within 1 business day.

Vision of Success

Ninety Days From Now,
Everything Changes.

Mike walked back into the gym for the first time in a year. Karen got on the floor to play with her granddaughter without thinking twice about her knee.

They're not just out of pain. They feel strong. They know what to do. And they're not worried about the next setback the same way they used to be.

Read Their Stories →

Every Month You Wait…

  • → You lose a little more strength and range of motion
  • → The confidence to move freely slips further away
  • → The trip, the season, the grandkid — they don't wait
  • → Most people who say "I'll deal with it later" are still saying it two years from now

Your Best Movement Years
Are Still Ahead of You.

Physical Therapy · Muscle Forward Approach

Strength Is the Treatment.
Not the Afterthought.

Most PT clinics treat your pain and send you home. We rebuild the strength that keeps you from coming back.

Schedule Your First Visit → ✦ Book a Free Movement Screen
💪
The Muscle Forward Approach

Why We Build Strength
Into Every Treatment Plan

Traditional PT focuses on pain reduction. That's step one — not the finish line. The Muscle Forward approach means we don't discharge you until you're actually stronger than you were before the injury.

🎯

Assess First

Every treatment starts with a full movement and strength evaluation — not just "where does it hurt?" We find the root cause so we're solving the right problem.

💪

Treat & Rebuild

Hands-on manual therapy plus progressive strength loading. Not exercises on a sheet — a real program that builds capacity and resilience over time.

🔁

Maintain & Prevent

Discharge isn't the end. We connect you to Active Strength, Active Stretch, and annual movement screens so the gains stick for life.

Why It Works

The Problem With
Standard PT

Most clinics treat symptoms until insurance runs out, then send you home. You feel better — for a while. But without rebuilding the underlying strength, the pain almost always comes back.

The Muscle Forward approach is built on one principle: your body heals better when it's strong. Pain management gets you to zero. Strength gets you to thriving.

This is why our patients come back and refer their friends and family — not because they had a good experience in the clinic, but because the results followed them home.

See What We Treat →
96%
of patients meet their primary goal by discharge
Maryland's First
Muscle-Forward PT Practice
Every plan. Every patient. Every visit.

The Active Life Difference

You Get a Team,
a Plan, and a Community.

Every visit, your PT is focused on you. But what sets us apart goes beyond the walls of the clinic.

🩺

Your PT Knows You

From your first eval to your final visit, your Doctor of PT stays current on your history, your progress, and your goals — so every session builds on the last one.

🗺️

A Long-Term Plan, Not Just Appointments

We don't just treat today's pain. We map out where you're going — through PT, into Active Strength or Stretch, and beyond discharge — so you're never starting from scratch again.

🤝

A Community That Holds You Up

Patients here become part of something. A staff that knows your name, a community that cheers your comeback, and a team that stays invested in your progress — not just your insurance visits.

  • Runner's knee

  • Total joint replacement

Active Life · Strength Training

Clinician-Supervised Strength.
Built for How You Actually Move.

Not a gym. Not a bootcamp. Strength training designed by Doctors of PT who know your history, your limits, and your goals.

Get Started →
🏋️
Meet Active Strength

Hear From Joe
About Our Strength Program

Joe walks you through what makes our strength program different — and why it's the bridge most patients are missing after PT discharge.

Active Strength — Intro with Joe
Video coming soon · Replace src with YouTube embed
Why Strength Matters

The Missing Piece
in Most Recovery Plans

Pain goes away when you rest. Strength only comes from loading. Most PT programs skip the loading part — which is exactly why the pain comes back.

🏋️

Progressive Loading

We systematically increase load and demand over time — the same way your body needs to adapt. No plateaus, no guesswork.

🩺

Clinical Oversight

Every program is designed by a Doctor of PT who knows your history. You're never loading a structure that isn't ready for it.

📈

Measurable Results

We track your progress and adjust. You'll know exactly how much stronger you're getting — and where to focus next.

Who It's For

Built for the Serious
and the Just-Getting-Started

Post-PT Patients

You finished PT — now let's build on it. Our strength programs bridge the gap between "discharged" and "actually strong."

Active Adults 50+

Strength training is the single best thing you can do for longevity, bone density, and independence. We make it safe and specific.

Athletes in Maintenance

Runners, cyclists, swimmers — we build the strength that keeps you training without breakdown.

Pre-Surgical Prep

The stronger you go in, the faster you come out. Prehab strength programs for upcoming joint replacements and procedures.

Get Started →
Know Where You Stand

Annual Strength Assessment

Before we build your program, we measure where you are. A full-body strength and movement baseline from a Doctor of PT — once a year, so every rep you do actually counts.

Common Questions

Strength Training FAQs

Do I need a PT referral to join Active Strength?
No referral needed. You can start Active Strength directly — we'll do an intake conversation to make sure the program is right for you. If we think you need PT first, we'll be upfront about that.
How is this different from going to a regular gym?
The programs are designed by Doctors of PT who know your injury history, movement patterns, and limitations. You're not following a generic plan — you're following one built around how your body actually works.
What equipment is used?
We use a combination of free weights, cables, resistance bands, and bodyweight — depending on your program and phase. The focus is on quality of movement and progressive overload, not the equipment itself.
How many sessions per week is typical?
Most Active Strength participants train 2–3 times per week. We'll talk through scheduling during your initial conversation so the program fits your life, not the other way around.
Is strength training covered by insurance?
Active Strength is typically a cash-pay service. We keep pricing straightforward and accessible — ask us about current rates when you call or book online. PT sessions are billed separately through insurance.
Get Started with Active Strength →
Strength · Annual Strength Assessment

You Can't Improve What
You Don't Measure.

A full-body strength and movement baseline from a Doctor of PT. Once a year, you know exactly where you stand — and what to do next.

Book Your Assessment →
📊
See What's Included

Joe Introduces the
Annual Strength Assessment

Watch Joe walk through exactly what happens during the assessment — and why knowing your baseline is the single most important step in any strength program.

Annual Strength Assessment — Intro with Joe
Video coming soon · Replace src with YouTube embed
What's Included

A Complete Picture of
Your Movement Health

01

Strength Testing

Bilateral strength comparisons across major muscle groups. We measure side-to-side deficits that predict injury before it happens.

02

Movement Screen

Functional movement patterns assessed by a DPT. We identify compensation patterns, mobility restrictions, and stability gaps.

03

Your Action Plan

You leave with a clear report and a prioritized list of what to work on — whether that's PT, Active Strength, or just some homework.

Expectations & Pricing

What You Can Expect
and What It Costs

What the Visit Looks Like

Duration

Plan for 60–75 minutes for your full assessment. This is not a rushed visit — we take the time to get the full picture.

What to Wear

Comfortable, athletic clothing you can move in. Shorts and a t-shirt work best. Bring your normal exercise shoes.

How Often

Annually — hence the name. We recommend reassessing every 12 months, or more frequently if you've had a major change in activity or recovery status.

After Your Assessment

You'll receive a written summary and a clear next-step recommendation — no vague "keep it up" feedback. Real data, real plan.

Assessment Pricing

Cash Pay
Annual Strength AssessmentCall for pricing
Assessment + Follow-up Plan SessionCall for pricing
Assessment + Styku Scan (bundled)Call for pricing
Assessments are cash pay and not billed through insurance. Call (410) 529-3303 or ask at your next visit for current pricing. We keep it simple and fair.

Why Annual

Your Body Changes.
Your Program Should Too.

An annual baseline catches small deficits before they become big injuries. It also shows you — in numbers — how much you've improved. That's motivation.

📋

Strength Assessment Resources

Access prep guides, what-to-expect docs, and follow-up exercise resources in the Patient Hub.

→
Book Your Annual Assessment →
Common Questions

Annual Assessment FAQs

Is the assessment covered by insurance?
The Annual Strength Assessment is a cash-pay service and is not billed through insurance. We keep pricing fair — call (410) 529-3303 for current rates.
Do I need to be a current PT patient to book an assessment?
No. The assessment is open to anyone — new patients, former patients, and people who've never been to Active Life before. It's designed to give you a clear picture of where you stand right now.
What happens after the assessment?
You leave with a written report and a prioritized recommendation — whether that's active strength, PT, home exercises, or a combination. There's no pressure to buy anything. You get the data and you decide.
How is this different from a physical therapy evaluation?
A PT eval is focused on diagnosing and treating a specific injury or condition. The Annual Strength Assessment is a performance and prevention screen — it's for people who are generally healthy but want to understand their strength baseline and catch problems before they become injuries.
Active Life · Active Stretch

Flexibility Isn't Just
Something You're Born With.

Assisted stretching and mobility work led by movement experts. Move better, recover faster — between PT sessions and for life.

Book a Stretch Session →
🧘
See It in Action

What Active Stretch
Looks Like in Practice

More than just holding a position — see how our clinicians work with you to access range of motion you can't reach on your own.

Active Stretch — Session Walkthrough
Video coming soon · Replace src with YouTube embed
What Is Active Stretch

More Than Just
Holding a Position

Active Stretch is facilitated flexibility work — a skilled clinician helps you access range of motion you can't get to on your own. Better range means less pain, better performance, and faster recovery.

🧘

Facilitated Stretching

Assisted techniques let you achieve and hold deeper ranges than solo stretching — without the compensation patterns that make self-stretching less effective.

🔓

Joint Mobility Work

Targeted joint mobilization and soft tissue work to unlock areas of restriction. Your hips, thoracic spine, and ankles thank you.

⚡

Recovery Acceleration

Between strength sessions, race days, or PT appointments — Active Stretch keeps tissue quality high and soreness low.

Who Benefits

For Anyone Who Wants
to Keep Moving Well

PT Patients Between Sessions

Keep the tissue work going between your PT appointments for faster progress.

Active Adults 50+

Mobility work is the best preventive maintenance you can do for joints and soft tissue as you age.

Runners & Cyclists

Tight hips and calves are a ticking clock. Regular stretch sessions prevent the overuse injuries that sideline training.

Post-PT Maintenance

Keep the gains you made in PT — and keep moving freely for the long haul.

Book a Stretch Session →
Patient Hub

Stretch Resources
in Your Patient Hub

Home stretching routines, mobility guides, and video walkthroughs — all in one place between sessions.

🔓

Active Stretch Resources

Access at-home mobility routines, stretching guides, and clinician-recommended exercises in the Patient Hub.

→
Common Questions

Active Stretch FAQs

How long is a typical Active Stretch session?
Sessions run 30–60 minutes depending on what you're working on. Most patients do one session per week, though some come more frequently during high training loads or after intense events.
Is this covered by insurance?
Active Stretch is a cash-pay service. We keep it accessible — call (410) 529-3303 for current pricing.
Is this the same as massage or chiropractic?
Different tools, different goals. Active Stretch is focused on improving joint range of motion and functional flexibility through guided, clinician-led movement — not passive tissue manipulation or spinal adjustments.
Do I need to be a PT patient first?
No. Active Stretch is open to anyone. If we think you'd benefit more from formal PT, we'll tell you — but you can start stretch sessions on their own.
Book Your First Stretch Session →

Patient Hub

Running Resources
in One Place

🏃

Running Program Resources

Home exercise library, gait cue guides, return-to-run protocols, and more — all in the Patient Hub.

→
Common Questions

Running Program FAQs

Do I need to be injured to use Active Run?
Not at all. Plenty of our running patients are healthy runners who want to get faster, more efficient, or prevent future injury. You don't need a problem to benefit from a gait analysis or strength program.
Is Active Run only at Jacksonville?
Active Run is based primarily at Jacksonville, but our running expertise extends across all three locations. Call us and we'll match you with the right location and clinician for your needs.
How does a gait analysis work?
We film you running on a treadmill and analyze your mechanics frame by frame. We look at foot strike, cadence, hip drop, arm swing, and other variables — then build a plan to address what we find.
How long is a typical return-to-run program?
It depends on your injury and starting point. Most return-to-run progressions run 6–12 weeks. We build them specifically to your goal race, training history, and current capacity — not a generic calendar.
Book a Running Evaluation →

Performance Therapies · Dry Needling

Release the Tension
That's Holding You Back.

Evidence-based trigger point dry needling targeting muscle knots and neural tension — reducing pain and restoring movement fast.

Book a Session →
⚡
See How It Works

Dry Needling
Explained

Not acupuncture — watch how our clinicians use dry needling to target trigger points, reset muscle tension, and reduce pain in ways that passive treatments can't reach.

Dry Needling — What to Expect
Video coming soon · Replace src with YouTube embed
What Is Dry Needling

Not Acupuncture.
Better Explained as Precision Muscle Reset.

Dry needling uses thin filament needles to target trigger points — tight, hypersensitive spots in muscle tissue that create local and referred pain. When we hit them right, the muscle releases, blood flow improves, and pain drops.

🎯

Trigger Point Release

Direct targeting of myofascial trigger points to break the pain-tension cycle and restore normal muscle function.

⚡

Fast-Acting Relief

Most patients notice significant improvement within 1–3 sessions. Combined with active PT, results compound quickly.

🔬

Evidence-Based

Strong research support for neck pain, low back pain, headaches, shoulder dysfunction, and athletic performance.

Conditions That Respond Well

Common Uses for
Dry Needling

  • → Chronic neck pain & headaches
  • → Low back pain & sciatica
  • → Shoulder & rotator cuff pain
  • → IT band & hip flexor tightness
  • → Calf & Achilles tightness
  • → Tennis & golfer's elbow
  • → Post-surgical scar tissue
  • → Athletic performance & recovery
What to Expect & Pricing

Transparent Expectations
Before You Book

What a Session Looks Like

Session Length

Dry needling is typically added into your PT session — it's not a standalone 60-minute visit. Needling itself takes 10–20 minutes depending on how many areas are treated.

How Often

Most patients receive dry needling 1–2 times per week during active PT. As your tissue responds and symptoms improve, frequency typically decreases. Most people see meaningful results within 3–5 sessions.

How It Feels

You may feel a local twitch response — a brief involuntary muscle contraction that's a good sign. Some mild soreness for 24–48 hours after is normal and expected, similar to a hard workout.

What to Do After

Stay hydrated. Gentle movement is encouraged. Avoid intense exercise the same day. Most patients are back to normal activity the following day.

Dry Needling Pricing

Add-on Service
Dry Needling (added to PT session)Billed through insurance
Standalone Dry Needling SessionCall for pricing
Dry needling performed during PT is typically billed through your insurance under your regular PT benefits. Standalone sessions are cash pay. Call (410) 529-3303 or ask your PT to confirm what's covered under your plan.
Common Questions

Dry Needling FAQs

Is dry needling the same as acupuncture?
No. Acupuncture is rooted in traditional Chinese medicine and focuses on energy channels. Dry needling is a Western, evidence-based technique performed by licensed PTs that targets specific muscle trigger points to release tension and reduce pain. Same tool, entirely different philosophy and application.
Does it hurt?
The needle itself is very thin and insertion causes minimal discomfort. You may feel a local twitch response — a brief cramp or deep ache — when the needle contacts a trigger point. That response is actually a sign it's working. Post-session soreness for 24–48 hours is normal.
How many sessions do I need?
Most patients notice meaningful improvement within 3–5 sessions. Chronic conditions or complex cases may take longer. We'll reassess as you go and adjust frequency based on how your tissue responds.
Who performs the dry needling?
Dry needling at Active Life is performed by licensed Doctors of Physical Therapy who have completed specific post-graduate training and certification in dry needling technique.
Is it safe?
Yes. Dry needling is a well-researched and widely used technique in physical therapy. We use sterile, single-use needles and follow strict safety protocols. Side effects are typically limited to minor soreness after the session.
Schedule Dry Needling →
Performance Therapies · Class 4 Therapeutic Laser

Deep Tissue Healing.
Accelerated.

The most powerful photobiomodulation in outpatient PT. Reduces pain, drives cellular healing, and gets you back faster.

Book a Session →
🔬
See It in Action

Class 4 Laser Therapy
Explained

Watch how therapeutic laser works at the cellular level — and why it's the fastest tool we have for reducing deep tissue inflammation and accelerating recovery.

Class 4 Laser Therapy — How It Works
Video coming soon · Replace src with YouTube embed
How It Works

Light That Goes Where
Other Treatments Can't

Class 4 laser delivers therapeutic photons deep into tissue — stimulating cellular repair, reducing inflammation at the source, and accelerating the body's natural healing processes without drugs or needles.

🔴

Deep Tissue Penetration

Class 4 power reaches deeper tissue layers than cold laser or low-level therapy — treating muscle, tendon, and joint structures effectively.

🔬

Cellular Repair Activation

Photobiomodulation stimulates ATP production in cells, accelerating the natural repair cycle and reducing recovery time.

💊

Drug-Free Pain Relief

Immediate pain reduction through neural effects combined with long-term healing through tissue repair. No side effects.

Conditions Treated

What Laser Therapy
Works Best For

  • → Tendinopathies (Achilles, patellar, rotator cuff)
  • → Arthritis — knee, hip, shoulder
  • → Plantar fasciitis
  • → Post-surgical tissue healing
  • → Muscle strains & tears
  • → Nerve pain & neuropathy
  • → Chronic inflammation
  • → Wound healing acceleration
  • → Lymphedema management
What to Expect & Pricing

Before You Come In:
What You Should Know

What a Session Looks Like

Session Length

A laser session takes 8–15 minutes depending on the area being treated and dosage. It's typically added to your PT session — not a separate appointment.

How Often

Acute conditions: typically 3x/week for 2–3 weeks. Chronic conditions may require 6–12 sessions. We'll set clear expectations based on your diagnosis and response.

How It Feels

Most patients feel a gentle, warming sensation. There's no pain — many describe it as relaxing. You may notice reduced pain and improved mobility within 24–48 hours of your first session.

What to Avoid

Avoid applying heat to the treated area for a few hours post-treatment. Don't use anti-inflammatory medications immediately before sessions, as they can blunt the healing response.

Laser Therapy Pricing

Add-on Service
Laser (added to PT session)Billed through insurance
Standalone Laser SessionCall for pricing
Laser Package (prepaid sessions)Call for pricing
Laser therapy performed as part of PT is typically billable through insurance. Standalone sessions are cash pay. Call (410) 529-3303 to confirm what's covered under your specific plan before booking.
Common Questions

Laser Therapy FAQs

Is Class 4 laser the same as TENS or ultrasound?
No. TENS uses electrical stimulation for temporary pain relief. Ultrasound creates mechanical vibration in tissue. Class 4 laser uses specific wavelengths of light to stimulate cellular repair — it works at the biological level, not just the symptom level. They can complement each other, but they're very different tools.
How quickly will I feel results?
Many patients notice reduced pain and improved mobility within 24–48 hours of their first session. Results are cumulative — each session builds on the last. Acute conditions typically respond faster than chronic ones.
Is it safe? Are there any risks?
Class 4 laser is very safe when used properly. We follow strict protocols and use appropriate protective eyewear. There are some contraindications (certain cancers, pregnancy, some medications) — we screen for these before treatment.
What's the difference between Class 4 and "cold laser"?
Power. Class 4 therapeutic laser delivers significantly more energy per session than cold laser or low-level laser therapy (LLLT). Higher power means deeper tissue penetration and faster therapeutic dose delivery — which is why results tend to come more quickly.
Book Laser Therapy →
Performance Therapies · Muscle Scans by Styku

See Your Body the Way
Your PT Sees It.

3D body composition and muscle analysis. Know where your strengths are, where your gaps are, and exactly how you're changing over time.

Book a Styku Scan →
📡
What Styku Measures

A 3D Picture of
Your Physical Baseline

📐

Body Composition

Precise lean mass, fat mass, and regional body composition data. Not just a number on a scale — a map of what's actually there.

⚖️

Bilateral Symmetry

Side-to-side comparisons that identify muscle imbalances before they become injuries. A 15% deficit is a red flag most people never see coming.

📊

Progress Tracking

Scan at baseline, then again at 90 days. See exactly where you've gained, where you've lost, and where to focus next.

Combined Power

Styku + Annual Assessment
= The Full Picture

Pair your Styku scan with an Annual Strength Assessment for a comprehensive movement and composition baseline. This is the data-driven foundation for a program that actually gets you where you want to go.

See Annual Assessments →

Patient Hub · What to Expect

Your First Visit,
Explained.

No surprises. Here's exactly what happens from the moment you book to the moment you leave — and what comes next.

Your Journey

From First Contact
to First Results

01

Schedule & Intake

Book online or call (410) 529-3303. We'll gather basic insurance info and send you intake paperwork to complete before you arrive so your first hour is spent on you — not paperwork.

02

Your Evaluation

Your first visit is a full evaluation with a Doctor of PT. We'll go through your history, assess movement and strength, and identify the actual source of your problem — not just the symptoms.

03

Your Plan of Care

Before you leave your first visit, you'll have a clear plan. How many visits, how often, what the goals are, and what you need to do at home. No vague "let's see how it goes."

Good to Know

A Few Things That Make
Your Visits Better

What to Wear

Comfortable workout clothes. We'll be moving, assessing, and sometimes applying hands-on treatment — athletic shorts and a t-shirt work perfectly.

What to Bring

Insurance card, photo ID, and any relevant imaging (X-rays, MRI reports). If you have a referral or prescription for PT, bring that too — though most plans don't require one.

How Long Is Each Visit

Evaluations run 60–75 minutes. Follow-up visits are typically 45–60 minutes. Every minute is one-on-one with your therapist.

How Many Visits Will I Need

It depends on the condition and your goals. We'll give you a honest estimate at your evaluation — and we update the plan as you progress. No padding your schedule unnecessarily.

Patient Hub · Supplements by Thorne

Clinician-Curated Supplements.
Delivered to You.

Active Life's Thorne dispensary — supplements selected by our Doctors of PT. NSF Certified. Trusted by the Mayo Clinic and 100+ professional sports teams.

Shop Our Dispensary → View All Products
Why Thorne

Not All Supplements
Are Created Equal

Thorne is NSF Certified for Sport — tested for banned substances, verified for label accuracy, and trusted by more than 100 professional sports teams. When we recommend a supplement, we need to know exactly what's in it. Thorne delivers that.

🔬

Third-Party Tested

NSF Certified for Sport. Every product is independently tested for identity, potency, and purity.

🏥

Clinician Formulated

Developed with healthcare practitioners — the same line used by the Mayo Clinic and professional sports organizations.

📦

Personalized Recs

Your PT can recommend specific products based on your goals — recovery, sleep, joint health, protein, and more.

Commonly Recommended

What We Suggest
Most Often

  • → Creatine (strength & recovery)
  • → Collagen + Vitamin C (tendon & joint health)
  • → Omega-3 (inflammation reduction)
  • → Magnesium (sleep & muscle recovery)
  • → Vitamin D3 + K2 (bone health)
  • → Whey Protein (lean tissue support)

Charitable Giving

Work With Us

Patient Hub · Success Stories

Real People.
Real Results.

The goal was never just "less pain." It was getting back to the gym, playing with the grandkids, finishing the race. Here's what that looks like.

★★★★★
"I'd been to two other PT clinics before Active Life. This was the first time anyone actually asked me what I wanted to get back to — and then built a plan around that answer. Back at the gym, full strength."
M

Knee replacement rehab · Perry Hall

K
Karen T.
Hip & knee rehab · Lutherville
★★★★★
"I finished my first half marathon after ACL surgery. My PT at Active Life kept me honest about the timeline and pushed me when I was ready. Crossed the finish line stronger than I was before the injury."
J
James W.
ACL reconstruction · Jacksonville
"Three PTs at other places told me my shoulder would never be 100%. Active Life got me to 110%. I'm lifting more now than I was before the rotator cuff tear."
T
Tom H.
Rotator cuff rehab · Perry Hall
★★★★★
"I was scared to move for six months after my spinal fusion. The Active Life team rebuilt my confidence as much as my body. I'm hiking again. That wasn't supposed to happen."
D
Diane M.
Spinal fusion rehab · Lutherville
"Every visit was one-on-one. My therapist knew exactly where I was in the program every single time. That kind of attention is why this worked when nothing else did."
R
Robert S.
Chronic low back pain · Jacksonville

Physician Portal · Referral Partners

When Your Patients Need PT,
We Take It From There.

Fast appointments, thorough documentation, a team that follows through. Your patients come back stronger — and return to you with real results.

Submit a Referral → Call (410) 529-3303
Why Refer to Active Life

A PT Partner That
Makes You Look Good

⚡

Same-Week Scheduling

Most patients are seen within 3–5 business days of referral. No weeks-long waitlists. Your patient gets moving fast.

📋

Thorough Documentation

Evaluation summaries, progress notes, and discharge reports sent to your office. You stay in the loop without chasing records.

🩺

Doctoral-Level Clinicians

Every patient is evaluated and treated by a Doctor of Physical Therapy. Your patient gets a clinician who knows their case — not someone reading off a sheet.

📞

Direct Communication

Questions about a patient's progress? Call the clinic directly. You'll talk to the therapist treating them, not a front desk script.

💪

Muscle Forward Outcomes

We don't just treat pain — we rebuild strength. Your patients come back to you stronger than before the injury, not just symptom-free.

📍

3 Convenient Locations

Perry Hall, Lutherville, and Jacksonville — covering Baltimore County so your patients can always find a location that works for them.

Send Your Patient
Our Way

Fill out the form below or fax to (410) 529-3303. A member of our team will contact the patient within 1 business day to schedule.

About · Active Life Physical Therapy

Built on One Belief:
Strong People Don't Stop Moving.

Founded in 2002. Three locations in Baltimore County. One mission — rebuild the strength that keeps you doing the things you love, for life.

Team Photo
Our Story

Why We Built
Active Life PT

Active Life Physical Therapy was founded in 2002 with a simple observation: most physical therapy stops when the pain stops. That's not the finish line — it's barely the halfway point.

We built something different. The Muscle Forward approach puts strength at the center of every treatment plan. We don't discharge people until they're actually stronger than they were before the injury.

Over twenty years later, we operate three clinics across Baltimore County, see 32,000+ patient visits a year, and still treat every patient one-on-one with a Doctor of Physical Therapy. That hasn't changed, and it won't.

Founded 20023 Locations32,000+ Visits / YearMuscle Forward
Company Leadership

The People Leading
Active Life PT

A tight leadership team with deep roots in Baltimore County — focused on building a physical therapy company that patients trust and clinicians are proud to be part of.

Joe Palmer

CEO / President / Founder

Joe founded Active Life Physical Therapy and has grown it from a single clinic to a three-location company serving Baltimore County. As CEO, CMO, and Chief Growth Officer, he drives strategy, culture, and the Active Life brand — including the Muscle Forward approach that defines how the company treats patients.

Mick Foley, DPT

Chief Administrative Officer · Perry Hall Director

Doctor of Physical Therapy who runs day-to-day operations, technology, HR, and process across all three locations — while also directing the Perry Hall clinic. Mick is the operational engine of Active Life and the architect of its systems and team culture.

Christina Penny

Director of Clinical Operations · Clinic Director, Jacksonville

Christina oversees clinical standards and consistency across all three Active Life locations, directs the Active Run program, and leads the Jacksonville clinic. She's the clinical compass that keeps quality high as the company grows.

MW

Mike Wah, DPT

Physical Therapist · All Locations

Experienced clinician and key contributor to Active Life's clinical culture and patient outcomes across all three locations.

Our Values

What We Actually
Believe In

Strength Is the Treatment

Not the afterthought. Every plan we build includes a strength component because that's where lasting results come from.

One-on-One Is Non-Negotiable

You get a Doctor of PT for your entire session. Not an aide, not a tech, not a clipboard exercise sheet.

Honest Timelines

We tell you what's realistic — not what you want to hear. That's how trust gets built and goals actually get met.

Community First

We treat Baltimore County, and Baltimore County is home. We stay involved — in schools, on race courses, in the neighborhoods where our patients live.

Your Results.

Careers · Join Active Life PT

Build Something That
Lasts. With Us.

A PT company where clinicians do their best work — one-on-one, Muscle Forward, with motivated patients and outcomes that actually mean something.

Apply Now →
Why Active Life

What Makes This Place
Different to Work At

🎯

Your Patient, Your Plan

You own your patient's plan from eval to discharge. You're not just executing someone else's protocol — you're the clinician. That's how good outcomes happen and how great PTs grow.

💪

Muscle Forward Culture

We've built a clinical culture around strength. You'll have the space to actually rehabilitate people — not just manage them.

📈

Room to Grow

Three locations and growing. Strong performers get leadership paths, mentorship, and a real stake in where this company goes.

🤝

Collaborative Team

Small enough to know each other, big enough to have real resources. The clinical conversations here are the best part of the job.

🏡

Baltimore County Roots

Care Guide · Knee & Lower Extremity

Knee Pain Doesn't Have to
Be Your New Normal.

Whether you're recovering from surgery or dealing with chronic pain, our Doctors of PT will find the root cause and rebuild the strength that protects your knee for life.

Schedule Your Evaluation → ✦ Book a Free Movement Screen
🦵
What Care Looks Like

Your Knee. Our Plan.
A Real Path Forward.

▶

Hear From Our Team

Joe, Mick, and Christina walk you through exactly what knee and lower extremity care looks like at Active Life — from eval to discharge and beyond.

Conditions We Treat

Knee & Lower Extremity
Conditions

🦵

Knee Replacement Rehab

Post-surgical rehab that gets you walking, climbing stairs, and back to life — not just cleared by your surgeon. We work alongside your surgical team for the best outcomes.

⚡

ACL & Meniscus Recovery

From the day after surgery to return-to-sport clearance — progressive strength and stability work that follows the evidence, not just a calendar.

🏃

IT Band & Runner's Knee

Overuse injuries that sideline runners and cyclists. We address the hip and core weakness driving the problem — not just the knee itself.

🦶

Plantar Fasciitis & Ankle

Heel and ankle pain that makes every step hurt. Manual therapy plus progressive calf and foot strengthening to get you back on your feet — for real this time.

🩺

Patellar Tendinopathy

Knee pain that flares with stairs, squatting, or sport. Loading the tendon correctly — not resting it — is the evidence-based answer. We build that program for you.

🔧

Ankle Sprains & Instability

Chronic ankle rolling is a strength and balance problem, not bad luck. We address the deficit so it doesn't keep happening.

Common Questions

Frequently Asked
About Knee Care

How long does knee replacement rehab take?
Most patients are walking independently within 2–4 weeks and reach full recovery in 3–6 months. The timeline depends on your strength going in, the type of replacement, and how consistently you work your program. We track your progress every visit.
Do I need a referral to start PT for my knee?
In Maryland, you can see a Doctor of Physical Therapy directly — no referral required. Most insurance plans cover PT without one. We can verify your benefits before your first visit.
I've had PT before and my knee pain came back. Why?
This is the most common thing we hear. Standard PT often stops when pain stops — not when you're actually strong again. The Muscle Forward approach means we don't discharge you until the underlying strength deficit is addressed. That's what keeps the pain from coming back.
Can I come in for a free movement screen before committing to treatment?
Yes — that's exactly what a free movement screen is for. 20 minutes with one of our Doctors of PT. We assess what's going on, explain our findings clearly, and tell you exactly what we'd recommend. No charge, no pressure.
Which location should I go to for knee care?
All three of our locations — Perry Hall, Lutherville, and Jacksonville — treat knee and lower extremity conditions. Choose the one that's most convenient for you. If you have a specific question about services at a particular clinic, give us a call at (410) 529-3303.
Knee & Lower Extremity

Ready to Stop Managing It
and Start Fixing It?

Care Guide · Back & Spine

Back Pain Is Common.
Living With It Isn't Mandatory.

Low back pain, sciatica, disc issues — we treat the root cause and rebuild the strength that actually protects your spine long-term.

Schedule Your Evaluation → ✦ Book a Free Movement Screen
🧠
What Care Looks Like

Your Spine. Our Plan.
Less Pain, More Life.

▶

Hear From Our Team

Joe, Mick, and Christina explain what back and spine care looks like at Active Life — and why strengthening the right muscles is the most important thing you can do for a lasting recovery.

Watch on YouTube →

VIDEO COMING SOON — Search "Active Life Physical Therapy Back Pain" on YouTube

Conditions We Treat

Back & Spine
Conditions

🔙

Low Back Pain

The #1 reason people come to us. We find out why it's happening — weak core, poor movement patterns, disc issues — and fix the actual problem instead of chasing the pain.

💾

Herniated & Bulging Discs

Conservative management with real results. McKenzie-based directional preference work combined with core strengthening. Most disc patients avoid surgery when they get the right PT.

⚡

Sciatica & Nerve Pain

That shooting, burning pain down the leg. We identify whether it's a disc, piriformis, or nerve mobility issue — and treat the right thing.

🏗️

Spinal Stenosis

Narrowing in the spine doesn't mean you're stuck. Flexion-based movement, strength work, and targeted manual therapy can dramatically improve function and reduce symptoms.

↕️

Neck Pain & Stiffness

Headaches, tightness, restricted rotation — cervical spine issues that don't resolve on their own. Manual therapy, dry needling, and targeted strengthening that actually changes things.

🔧

Post-Spinal Surgery Rehab

Fusion, laminectomy, discectomy — we partner with your surgeon and get you through recovery with the right progression of mobility and strength work.

Download Free PDF →
Common Questions

Frequently Asked
About Back & Spine Care

Will PT help if I have a herniated disc?
For most people, yes. Research consistently shows that conservative PT is as effective as surgery for herniated discs in the long run — with far fewer risks. We use directional preference work, manual therapy, and core strengthening to take pressure off the disc and rebuild the support system around it.
How long until I feel better?
Most patients notice meaningful improvement within 2–4 weeks. Full resolution of acute low back pain usually takes 6–8 weeks. Chronic cases can take longer, but consistent improvement is the norm — not the exception — when the right structures are being treated.
My MRI shows a disc bulge. Does that mean surgery?
Not usually. Studies show that many adults without any back pain have disc bulges on MRI — they're often incidental findings. What matters is how you move, how strong you are, and what your symptoms are. That's what we assess. We'll tell you honestly if we think you need a surgical consultation.
Can I book a free movement screen just to understand what's going on?
Absolutely. That's exactly what it's for. One of our Doctors of PT will do a brief movement assessment, explain what they find, and give you a clear next step — at no cost, no commitment.
Back & Spine

Stop Waiting for It to
Go Away on Its Own.

Care Guide · Shoulder & Upper Extremity

Your Shoulder Shouldn't
Limit What You Love.

Rotator cuff, frozen shoulder, post-surgical rehab — we restore full function and build the strength that keeps it from coming back.

Schedule Your Evaluation → ✦ Book a Free Movement Screen
🦾
▶

Hear From Our Team

Our clinicians walk you through shoulder care — what your first visit looks like, what typical recovery involves, and what we do differently.

Watch on YouTube →

VIDEO COMING SOON — Search "Active Life Physical Therapy Shoulder" on YouTube

Conditions We Treat

Shoulder & Upper Extremity
Conditions

🔄

Rotator Cuff Tears & Tendinitis

The most common shoulder injury — and one where PT can prevent surgery or maximize surgical outcomes. Progressive loading of the cuff is the standard of care, and we do it right.

🔧

Shoulder Replacement Rehab

Total and reverse shoulder replacements require precise progressions. We follow evidence-based protocols while customizing to your timeline, surgeon's restrictions, and specific goals.

❄️

Frozen Shoulder

Adhesive capsulitis can be stubborn — but with the right manual therapy and progressive joint mobilization, most patients recover full range. We've seen it many times.

🎾

Tennis & Golfer's Elbow

Tendinopathy of the forearm extensors and flexors. Loading these tendons — not resting them — is what the research supports. We build the right program.

💻

Carpal Tunnel & Wrist

Nerve gliding, manual therapy, and ergonomic guidance that reduce compression without surgery in many cases. Conservative management is the first line — and it works.

✋

Hand & Finger Injuries

From post-surgical hand rehab to tendon injuries and fracture recovery — we treat the whole upper extremity, not just the big joints.

Timeline, stages, and what PT does to accelerate each phase of recovery.

Download Free PDF →
Common Questions

Frequently Asked
About Shoulder Care

Can PT fix a rotator cuff tear without surgery?
For partial tears and many full-thickness tears, conservative PT is highly effective. For large, complete tears in younger active adults, surgery may be the right call — but PT should always be the first step. We'll tell you honestly after your evaluation what we think gives you the best outcome.
How long does frozen shoulder take to heal?
Frozen shoulder typically progresses through three stages over 1–3 years without treatment. With PT — specifically manual joint mobilization and targeted stretching — most patients recover full range in 3–6 months. Starting early matters.
I had shoulder surgery 6 months ago and still have pain. Is that normal?
It depends on what surgery you had and what "pain" means. Some stiffness and weakness at 6 months is common. Significant pain or loss of function at 6 months warrants evaluation — there may be something we can still do to improve your outcome. A free movement screen is a good place to start.
Do I need a referral from my doctor?
No. In Maryland you can see a Doctor of PT directly. Most insurance plans cover PT without a referral. Call us at (410) 529-3303 and we can verify your benefits before your first visit.
Shoulder & Upper Extremity

You Deserve a Shoulder
That Actually Works.

Care Guide · Hip & Pelvis

Your Hips Drive Everything.
Let's Get Them Right.

Hip replacement recovery, impingement, SI joint pain — we address the full hip-pelvis system and build the strength that protects it for years to come.

Schedule Your Evaluation → ✦ Book a Free Movement Screen
🦴
▶

Hear From Our Team

Our team walks through what hip and pelvis care looks like — and why rebuilding hip strength is one of the highest-return things you can do for your body.

Watch on YouTube →

VIDEO COMING SOON — Search "Active Life Physical Therapy Hip" on YouTube

Conditions We Treat

Hip & Pelvis
Conditions

🔧

Hip Replacement Rehab

We guide you through every phase — from post-op precautions through return to full function. Hip replacement patients who do their PT get dramatically better long-term outcomes.

⚡

Hip Impingement (FAI)

Femoro-acetabular impingement doesn't always require surgery. Targeted hip strengthening and movement retraining can reduce pain and restore function for many patients.

🔴

Bursitis & Tendinopathy

Greater trochanteric bursitis, hip flexor tendinopathy — conditions that make sitting, sleeping, and walking painful. Loading the right structures turns these around.

🔑

SI Joint Dysfunction

Sacroiliac joint pain that's often misdiagnosed as low back pain. We identify it correctly and treat it with manual therapy and stabilization work specific to the SI joint.

🪑

Piriformis Syndrome

Deep gluteal pain that mimics sciatica. Dry needling, manual release, and targeted hip strengthening to resolve the compression and keep it from coming back.

💪

Groin Strains

Adductor strains are common in active adults and athletes. Progressive loading through the right ranges gets you back safely and reduces re-injury risk.

Download Free PDF →
Common Questions

Frequently Asked
About Hip Care

How long after hip replacement before I can exercise normally?
Most patients return to low-impact activity (walking, cycling, swimming) within 6–8 weeks. Higher-impact activities take 3–6 months. The key is following the right strength progression — which is exactly what PT provides. Patients who complete their PT return to activity faster and with fewer setbacks.
Is my hip pain coming from my hip or my back?
This is one of the most common diagnostic puzzles we see. Hip and spine problems frequently mimic each other. A thorough movement assessment — which we do at every eval — separates the two. The answer changes everything about how we treat it.
Can you help with hip pain even if I'm not having surgery?
Absolutely. Many hip conditions — bursitis, impingement, tendinopathy, SI joint dysfunction — respond very well to conservative PT. Surgery is often avoidable when the right muscles are strengthened and movement patterns are corrected early.
Hip & Pelvis

Strong Hips Change Everything.
Let's Build Yours.

Care Guide · Sports & Overuse Injuries

You Didn't Train This Hard
to Sit on the Sidelines.

From runner's knee to ACL tears — we get you back in the game safely, with the strength to stay there.

Schedule Your Evaluation → ✦ Book a Free Movement Screen
🏃
▶

Hear From Our Team

Joe, Mick, and Christina talk about how we approach sports and overuse injuries — and why most athletes are undertreated until something breaks down completely.

Watch on YouTube →

VIDEO COMING SOON — Search "Active Life Physical Therapy Sports Injuries" on YouTube

Conditions We Treat

Sports & Overuse
Conditions

🏃

Runner's Knee & IT Band

Patellofemoral pain and IT band syndrome are hip and biomechanics problems as much as knee problems. We fix the source — not just where it hurts.

🦴

Stress Fractures

Safe return-to-load programs that respect healing timelines and progressively rebuild the tolerance that prevents recurrence. We work alongside your physician on clearance.

💥

Muscle Strains & Tears

Grade I through III tears of hamstrings, quads, calves, and more. Optimal loading at each stage of healing is what the research says works — and it's what we do.

🦵

Shin Splints (MTSS)

Medial tibial stress syndrome that keeps flaring up. We identify the training load and biomechanical factors and fix them — so you can keep running.

⚡

Achilles Tendinopathy

The most evidence-supported treatment for Achilles problems is progressive tendon loading — not rest. We build the exact program that turns this around.

🏅

Return-to-Sport Programs

Criteria-based return-to-sport — not calendar-based. We test whether you're actually ready, not just whether enough time has passed. That's the difference between going back strong and going back again.

The real criteria for safe return to competitive sport — beyond just "it doesn't hurt anymore."

Download Free PDF →
Common Questions

Frequently Asked
About Sports Injuries

Should I rest or keep training through an overuse injury?
It depends on the injury, the severity, and your training load. "Rest" is rarely the complete answer — and continuing the exact same training is rarely right either. What almost always works: a modified load that keeps you active while allowing healing. We figure that out at your evaluation.
Can I train while in PT?
In most cases, yes. We build your PT program around your training — not the other way around. Our goal is to keep you as active as safely possible while you recover. We'll tell you clearly what to modify and what to avoid.
How do I know when I'm actually ready to return to sport?
Time alone isn't a good indicator. We use strength tests, movement quality assessments, and sport-specific functional testing to determine actual readiness — not just calendar dates. This is what prevents re-injury.
Do you work with adult recreational athletes, or just competitive ones?
Both — and honestly, recreational athletes are our specialty. The weekend warrior who wants to run their next race, finish the season, keep hiking with their kids. You don't have to be elite for us to take your goals seriously.
Sports & Overuse Injuries

Get Back to the Sport
You Love — Stronger.

Care Guide · Post-Surgical Rehab

Surgery Got You Here.
We'll Get You the Rest of the Way.

Total joints, ligament reconstructions, spinal surgery — the outcome of your operation depends heavily on what happens after. That's where we come in.

Schedule Your Evaluation → ✦ Book a Free Movement Screen
🔧
▶

Hear From Our Team

Joe, Mick, and Christina explain what post-surgical rehab looks like at Active Life — and why the right PT partner makes all the difference in your surgical outcome.

Watch on YouTube →

VIDEO COMING SOON — Search "Active Life Physical Therapy Post Surgical" on YouTube

Surgeries We Rehab

Post-Surgical Rehab
We Specialize In

🦴

Total Joint Replacement

Hip, knee, and shoulder replacements. We work with your surgeon's protocol and push you as fast as your healing allows — so you get your life back faster.

⚡

Ligament Reconstruction

ACL, PCL, MCL, and multi-ligament reconstructions. Evidence-based timelines with criteria-based milestones — not just counting weeks on a calendar.

🔭

Arthroscopic Procedures

Meniscus repairs, labral repairs (hip and shoulder), and debridement procedures. Each has specific healing constraints we understand and respect.

🏗️

Spinal Surgery Recovery

Lumbar fusion, laminectomy, discectomy, and cervical procedures. We coordinate with your spine surgeon and progress you carefully through each phase.

🔄

Rotator Cuff Repair

Rotator cuff surgery has well-established rehabilitation phases. We maximize each one — from passive range in weeks 1–6 to full strengthening at months 3–6.

🦾

Fracture & Trauma Rehab

Complex fractures of the upper and lower extremity — we restore full function through graduated mobilization, strengthening, and return-to-activity programming.

Download Free PDF →
Common Questions

Frequently Asked
About Post-Surgical Rehab

When should I start PT after surgery?
It depends on the surgery and your surgeon's protocol. For many joint replacements, PT starts within days of surgery. For tendon and ligament repairs, there may be a period of immobilization first. We coordinate directly with your surgical team and follow their timeline — while pushing the edges safely when you're ready.
Will my insurance cover post-surgical PT?
Almost always, yes. Post-surgical rehabilitation is typically covered under most insurance plans. The number of visits and copays vary by plan. We can verify your benefits before your first visit so there are no surprises.
My surgeon said I'm done with PT but I don't feel back to normal. What should I do?
This is more common than most people think. Surgical discharge and PT discharge don't have to be the same thing. You can continue PT or transition to Active Strength programming to get the rest of the way back. Call us at (410) 529-3303 — we'll figure out the right next step.
Can I do PT before surgery to improve my outcome?
Absolutely. Pre-surgical PT (prehab) is one of the highest-return things you can do. Stronger patients before surgery consistently have faster recoveries, less pain, and better long-term outcomes. Even 4–6 weeks of prehab makes a measurable difference.
Post-Surgical Rehab

Your Surgery Was Step One.
Let's Finish the Job.
